steps.title: Spot the opposition between the two maps
steps.content: Topographic maps measure elevation above sea level; bathymetric maps measure elevation below the sea. The two map types are directly opposed on the same dimension.

steps.title: Choose the transition that marks the contrast
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast. The blank needs a transition signaling that bathymetric maps are the counterpart to topographic maps.
  • Choice A — "afterward,": signals a later event in time, but no time sequence is described here, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "in conclusion,": signals a final summary of an argument; the passage is describing two map types, not wrapping up a case, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "for example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim; bathymetric maps aren't an example of topographic maps—they are their opposite, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "by contrast,": signals direct opposition between two things on the same dimension (above vs. below sea level), so fits. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
